chunks.text: The climate of our planet has been changing rapidly since the industrial revolution.
The global average temperature has already risen by about 1.
One of the main causes is the emission of carbon dioxide produced by the burning of fossil fuel.
The cutting of forests and the way of agriculture are also deeply involved.
As a result, sea levels rise, floods and droughts have become a frequent disaster
and typhoons have grown stronger than ever before.
Ecosystem are being destroyed, and many species are facing the threat of extinction.
In 1997, the Kyoto protocol was adopted
and each country was given a target for the reduction of greenhouse gas.
In 2015, the Paris agreement was reached
and the world set its goal of keeping the rise in temperature within 1.
Each country has begun to increase investment in renewable energy and to regulate the emission of cars and factories.
But climate change is no longer a problem that one country can solve by itself.
Without international cooperation, we cannot protect the future of the earth.
The achievement of a sustainable society is now a shared goal across generations.
